MINI MINUTES: The two following cases PTR-07-290534 In Re: Special Needs Trusts for Wiley Burns Trust and PTR-07-290535 In Re: Special Needs Trusts for Barbara Burns are being heard together.

Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch

Mr. Der addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to what is considered special needs. Mr Der stated that special needs are anything not paid by Medi-Cal and then he described how the expenses are being incurred. The Court is having issues with the $112.50 related to erroneous inventories and with both truts being small the fees should charged at 1% of the fair market value . The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-08-2013 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-25-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PTR-07-290535 In Re: Special Needs Trust for Barbara Burns.

Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

Mr. Der addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the large expenditures and the gift cards. The Court granted both petitions (signing the orders for the same in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Bloom.

Off Calendar May-06-2009 Continued To Jun-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 290535 (The Barbara Burns Special Needs Trust).

Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Joedda McDonald. Barbara Burns and Wiley Burns also present.

The Court referred to the First Account filed on December 15, 2008 on Wiley's trust in detail stating that there were many unexplained expenditures, that the receipts offered did not justify the expenditures, that the accounting was not being done properly and that it appeared that the bulk of the account assets were being kept in non interest bearing accounts. Similar issues occur with Barbara's trust. Because of the way expenditures have mounted, the Beneficiaries were told that the funds are not there to be spent as they wished and that they can only be spent in the manner in which the trust authorizes. The Court suspended Trustee Joedda McDonald's powers. The Trustee is to surrender all trust property within 24 hours. All funds on both matters are to be blocked immediately and no funds are to be removed without a Court order. Mr. Vann is to file a receipt for blocked accounts on both matters. Joedda McDonald must nominate a Successor Trustee and Counsel is to file the Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee by June 17, 2009. If the Trustee fails to nominate a Successor Trustee, the Court on it's own motion shall appoint a Successor Trustee under it's power set forth in Section 5.01 of the Trust. The acccounting petitions on both matters were continued to June 17,2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Ann Mendoza, CSR#6812. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Jackson.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-24-2009 Continued To Apr-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 290535 (The Barbara Burns Special Needs Trust).

Appearances: Dennis Vann for Joedda McDonald.

The Court addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes regarding unexplained expenditures. Mr. Vann stated that he filed a declaration and a stack of receipts on Friday. In addition, Mr. Vann stated that all of Wiley's clothes had been discarded, that both trust's were complicated, that his Client had heated discussions with the Beneficiaries with regard to their spending habits and that it would beneficial to have both of the Beneficiaries in Court. Both matters were continued to April 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow the Court to review the declaration. The Court wants assurance that Ms. McDonald will exercise good judgment for spending the trust funds for the Beneficiaries. Clothing expenses should be reasonable and at a store where minimal amounts will be spent. Ms. McDonald is not to reimburse herself for any expenses.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
